title: Identify and Correct Invalid HGNC Human Gene Symbols and MGI Mouse Gene Symbols
text: update symbols maps human mouse , download repository run: ./update.sh root directory. Note script uses “roxygen2” R library update documentation. Alternatively, can use updated maps without updating package, see ?getCurrentMaps.

what: HGNChelper: Identify and Correct Invalid HGNC Human Gene Symbols and MGI Mouse Gene Symbols — HGNChelper-package
title: HGNChelper: Identify and Correct Invalid HGNC Human Gene Symbols and MGI Mouse Gene Symbols — HGNChelper-package
text: Contains functions identifying correcting HGNC human gene symbols MGI mouse gene symbols converted date format Excel, withdrawn, aliased. Also contains functions reversibly converting HGNC symbols valid R names.

title: HGNChelper: Identify and Correct Invalid HGNC Human Gene Symbols and MGI Mouse Gene Symbols — HGNChelper-package

what: Identify outdated or Excel-mogrified gene symbols — checkGeneSymbols
title: Identify outdated or Excel-mogrified gene symbols — checkGeneSymbols
text: function identifies gene symbols outdated may mogrified Excel spreadsheet programs. output assigned variable, returns data.frame number rows input, second column indicating whether symbols valid third column corrected gene list.

title: Identify outdated or Excel-mogrified gene symbols — checkGeneSymbols
code: checkGeneSymbols(   x,   chromosome = NULL,   unmapped.as.na = TRUE,   map = NULL,   species = "human",   expand.ambiguous = FALSE )

title: Identify outdated or Excel-mogrified gene symbols — checkGeneSymbols
text: x character vector gene symbols check modified outdated values chromosome optional integer vector containing chromosome number gene provided argument x. length input x. Currently, argument implemented human gene cases. unmapped..na TRUE (default), unmapped symbols appear NA Suggested.Symbol column. FALSE, original unmapped symbol kept. map Specify want use default maps provided setting species equal "mouse" "human". Map can data.frame colnames identical c("Symbol", "Approved.Symbol"). default maps can updated running interactive example . species character vector length 1, either "human" (default) "mouse". NULL, anything "human" "mouse", map argument must provided. expand.ambiguous FALSE (default), genes multiple mapping map approved symbol correct one. TRUE, genes multiple/ambiguous mapping map symbols linked .

title: Identify outdated or Excel-mogrified gene symbols — checkGeneSymbols
text: function return data.frame number rows input, corrections possible map.

title: Identify outdated or Excel-mogrified gene symbols — checkGeneSymbols
code: library(HGNChelper)  ## Human human <- c("FN1", "TP53", "UNKNOWNGENE","7-Sep", "9/7", "1-Mar", "Oct4", "4-Oct",       "OCT4-PG4", "C19ORF71", "C19orf71") checkGeneSymbols(human) #> Maps last updated on: Sat Nov 16 10:35:32 2024 #> Warning: Human gene symbols should be all upper-case except for the 'orf' in open reading frames. The case of some letters was corrected. #> Warning: x contains non-approved gene symbols #>              x Approved   Suggested.Symbol #> 1          FN1     TRUE                FN1 #> 2         TP53     TRUE               TP53 #> 3  UNKNOWNGENE    FALSE               <NA> #> 4        7-Sep    FALSE            SEPTIN7 #> 5          9/7    FALSE            SEPTIN7 #> 6        1-Mar    FALSE MARCHF1 /// MTARC1 #> 7         Oct4    FALSE             POU5F1 #> 8        4-Oct    FALSE             POU5F1 #> 9     OCT4-PG4    FALSE           POU5F1P4 #> 10    C19ORF71    FALSE            TEKTIP1 #> 11    C19orf71    FALSE            TEKTIP1  ## Mouse mouse <- c("1-Feb", "Pzp", "A2m") checkGeneSymbols(mouse, species="mouse") #> Maps last updated on: Sat Nov 16 10:35:32 2024 #> Warning: x contains non-approved gene symbols #>       x Approved Suggested.Symbol #> 1 1-Feb    FALSE             Feb1 #> 2   Pzp     TRUE              Pzp #> 3   A2m     TRUE              A2m  ## expand.ambiguous  ## Human human <- "AAVS1" checkGeneSymbols(human, expand.ambiguous=FALSE) #> Maps last updated on: Sat Nov 16 10:35:32 2024 #>       x Approved Suggested.Symbol #> 1 AAVS1     TRUE            AAVS1 checkGeneSymbols(human, expand.ambiguous=TRUE) #> Maps last updated on: Sat Nov 16 10:35:32 2024 #>       x Approved   Suggested.Symbol #> 1 AAVS1     TRUE AAVS1 /// PPP1R12C  ## Mouse mouse <- c("Cpamd8", "Mug2") checkGeneSymbols(mouse, species = "mouse", expand.ambiguous = FALSE) #> Maps last updated on: Sat Nov 16 10:35:32 2024 #>        x Approved Suggested.Symbol #> 1 Cpamd8     TRUE           Cpamd8 #> 2   Mug2     TRUE             Mug2 checkGeneSymbols(mouse, species = "mouse", expand.ambiguous = TRUE) #> Maps last updated on: Sat Nov 16 10:35:32 2024 #>        x Approved Suggested.Symbol #> 1 Cpamd8     TRUE  Cpamd8 /// Mug2 #> 2   Mug2     TRUE  Mug2 /// Cpamd8  ## Updating the map if (interactive()) {     currentHumanMap <- getCurrentHumanMap()     checkGeneSymbols(human, map=currentHumanMap)      # You should save this if you are going to use it multiple times,        # then load it from file rather than burdening HGNC's servers.     save(hgnc.table, file="hgnc.table.rda", compress="bzip2")     load("hgnc.table.rda")     checkGeneSymbols(human, map=hgnc.table) }

what: Title function to identify Excel-mogrified gene symbols — findExcelGeneSymbols
title: Title function to identify Excel-mogrified gene symbols — findExcelGeneSymbols
text: function identifies gene symbols may mogrified Excel spreadsheet programs.  output assigned variable, returns vector length symbols mapped mapped.

title: Title function to identify Excel-mogrified gene symbols — findExcelGeneSymbols
code: findExcelGeneSymbols(   x,   mog.map = read.csv(system.file("extdata/mog_map.csv", package = "HGNChelper"), as.is =     TRUE),   regex = "impossibletomatch^" )

title: Title function to identify Excel-mogrified gene symbols — findExcelGeneSymbols
text: x Vector gene symbols check mogrified values mog.map Map known mogrifications.  dataframe two columns: original mogrified, containing correct incorrect symbols, respectively. regex Regular expression, recognized base::grep function called ignore.case=TRUE, identify mogrified symbols. default regex match anything. regex examples attempt match Excel-mogrified HGNC human gene symbols. necessary matches corresponding entry mog.map$mogrified; values x matched regex found mog.map$mogrified simply corrected.

title: Title function to identify Excel-mogrified gene symbols — findExcelGeneSymbols
text: return value function assigned variable, function return vector length input, corrections possible mog.map made.

title: Title function to identify Excel-mogrified gene symbols — findExcelGeneSymbols
code: ## Available maps from this package: human <- read.csv(system.file("extdata/mog_map.csv",                                package = "HGNChelper"), as.is=TRUE) mouse <- read.csv(system.file("extdata/HGNChelper_mog_map_MGI_AMC_2016_03_30.csv",                                package = "HGNChelper"), as.is=TRUE) ## This regex is based that provided by Zeeberg et al., ##  Mistaken Identifiers: Gene name errors can be introduced ## inadvertently when using Excel in bioinformatics.  BMC ##  Bioinformatics 2004, 5:80. re <- "[0-9]\\-(JAN|FEB|MAR|APR|MAY|JUN|JUL|AUG|SEP|OCT|NOV|DEC)|[0-9]\\.[0-9][0-9]E\\+[[0-9][0-9]" findExcelGeneSymbols(c("2-Apr", "APR2"), mog.map=human, regex=re) #> Warning: Transmogrified gene symbols found.  Returning the following corrections: 2-Apr to 2-Apr #> [1] "2-Apr" "APR2"  findExcelGeneSymbols(c("1-Feb", "Feb1"), mog.map=mouse) #> Warning: Transmogrified gene symbols found.  Returning the following corrections: 1-Feb to Feb1 #> [1] "Feb1" "Feb1"

what: Get the current maps for correcting gene symbols — getCurrentMaps
title: Get the current maps for correcting gene symbols — getCurrentMaps
text: Valid human mouse gene symbols can updated frequently. Use functions get current lists valid symbols, can use input map argument checkGeneSymbols. Make sure change default species="human" argument checkGeneSymbols mouse. Use getCurrentHumanMap HGNC human gene symbols https://www.genenames.org/ getCurrentMouseMap MGI mouse gene symbols https://www.informatics.jax.org/downloads/reports/MGI_EntrezGene.rpt.

title: Get the current maps for correcting gene symbols — getCurrentMaps
Get the current maps for correcting gene symbols — getCurrentMaps
text: data.frame can used map argument checkGeneSymbols function

title: Get the current maps for correcting gene symbols — getCurrentMaps
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ## human new.hgnc.table <- getCurrentHumanMap() checkGeneSymbols(c("3-Oct", "10-3", "tp53"), map=new.hgnc.table)  ## mouse new.mouse.table <- getCurrentMouseMap() ## Set species to NULL or "mouse"  checkGeneSymbols(c("Gm46568", "1-Feb"), map=new.mouse.table, species="mouse") } # }

what: All current and withdrawn HGNC gene symbols and Excel-mogrified symbols — hgnc.table
title: All current and withdrawn HGNC gene symbols and Excel-mogrified symbols — hgnc.table
text: data.frame first column providing gene symbol known alias (including withdrawn symbols), second column providing approved HGNC human gene symbol. Symbol: valid, Excel-mogrified, withdrawn symbols Approved.Symbol: Approved symbols

title: All current and withdrawn HGNC gene symbols and Excel-mogrified symbols — hgnc.table
All current and withdrawn HGNC gene symbols and Excel-mogrified symbols — hgnc.table
text: object class data.table (inherits data.frame) 103939 rows 3 columns.

title: All current and withdrawn HGNC gene symbols and Excel-mogrified symbols — hgnc.table
text: Extracted https://storage.googleapis.com/public-download-files/hgnc/tsv/tsv/hgnc_complete_set.txt system.file("extdata/mog_map.csv", package="HGNChelper")

what: Title function to *reversibly* convert HGNC gene symbols to valid R names. — symbolToR
title: Title function to *reversibly* convert HGNC gene symbols to valid R names. — symbolToR
text: function reversibly converts HGNC gene symbols valid R names prepending "symbol.", making following substitutions: "-" "hyphen", "@" "ampersand", "/" "forwardslash".
